spec: Myco v0.9 crypto primitives (L1_SCHEMA §2.1 Merkle DAG + L1_SKIN §2 HMAC envelope_digest)
description: Cross-language test vectors for the cryptographic primitives used by the substrate kernel + anchor_client + operator_bindings. Drift between implementations = L1_HARD_RULES C18 canonical_bytes_render_drift extended to crypto layer (CRITICAL).

merkle_hash.spec: merkle_hash(parents: list[NodeHash], content: bytes) = BLAKE3(parent_count_be_u64 || concat(parents) || content). Per L1_SCHEMA §2.1 + pass-3 mycoparasite-2: the parent_count prefix prevents ambiguity attacks between '1 parent + 32-byte content' and '2 parents + 0-byte content' encodings.

vectors.name: merkle_one_parent_all_ab
vectors._description: Single parent (all 0xab bytes), empty content. Verifies parent_count=1 + parent_bytes flow without content interference.

vectors.name: merkle_two_parents
vectors._description: Two parents + content. Order of parents matters: 0xab... before 0xcd... (declared order).

vectors.name: merkle_three_parents_canonical_bytes
vectors._description: Three parents + 4-byte content. Exercises both parent_count varint-boundary-adjacent value (3) and a non-trivial parent count.

hmac_sha256.spec: hmac_sign(key: bytes, msg: bytes) = HMAC-SHA256(key, msg). Per L1_SKIN §2: envelope_digest = HMAC(operator_token, canonical_envelope_fields || payload). Empty key forbidden — caller must use the operator_token.

vectors.name: hmac_simple_key_empty_msg
vectors._description: 3-byte key 'key', empty msg. Verifies behavior on minimal inputs.

vectors.name: hmac_32byte_key_msg
vectors._description: 32-byte key (operator_token-sized), 5-byte msg. Matches the L1_SKIN envelope_digest use case where key = OperatorToken (32 bytes).

vectors.name: hmac_long_key
vectors._description: 100-byte key (longer than SHA-256 block size of 64 bytes; HMAC must internally hash + pad). Tests the long-key code path.

empty_key_must_error._description: All implementations MUST reject empty HMAC keys (CryptoError::HmacEmptyKey in Rust; equivalent in Python/TS).
